scores.materiality_reasoning: Influence over linguistic tradition is primarily symbolic/cultural, though it impacts social status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the broad use of language and dialect preservation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Native speech patterns are generally lifelong characteristics.
scores.immutability_reasoning: The group 'Women' is biologically and socially fixed by sex.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Socrates describes the speech patterns of contemporary Athenian women and ancestors.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a general observation of linguistic behavior, though framed in a dialogue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being female does not inherently mean one preserves archaic speech.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to education is a social practice with tangible developmental outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular stage of a music/rhetoric curriculum (rhythm).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the period of one's education/training.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being a student is an episodic life-stage role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: References the standard contemporary pedagogical method in Greece.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes an established institutional practice ('just as those who undertake...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a student does not automatically define the specific sequence of rhythmic study.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to communicate is a concrete legal/social necessity for personhood.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of social interaction and expression.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Muteness is typically a lifelong condition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by permanent physical disability.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to how 'dumb people actually do' behave in the author's world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Observational fact regarding established non-verbal communication norms.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being mute doesn't define the specific resource of using hand/head signs.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Authority over language establishment is a concrete political/legal power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad institutional power over social semiotics.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of lawgiver usually spans a career or foundational period.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation requiring specific social status or appointment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the concept of the Nomothetes in Greek political life.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Philosophical discussion on the nature of an art/role.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A lawgiver's definition usually involves law, not necessarily 'naming'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Names as a tool for instruction are a structural social resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the entire mechanism of linguistic assignment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Role-bound but pertains to the durable impact of the legislated names.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupational role requiring appointment or recognition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to standard Greek conceptions of legislative roles.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Dialogue explores the definition and quality of the role.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lawgiving and 'artisan of names' are distinct concepts.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The 'right' to investigate is an intellectual/behavioral expectation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped to the specific activity of philosophical investigation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Specific to the life-stage of being 'young and in your prime'.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is immutable at any given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Directed by Socrates to Cratylus in their shared present.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Exhortative statement ('you must consider') rather than a report of a law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being young doesn't tautologically require 'thorough investigation'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to high-quality food and elite spaces has tangible material payoff.
scores.generality_reasoning: Scoped to the specific scenario of a dinner party substitution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A momentary benefit for a single occasion.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket/pauper status is durable but not immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes contemporary Greco-Roman social patronage and 'parasite' culture.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported dialogue reflecting standard social practices of the era.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being poor does not define being an invited dinner substitute.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Social obligations to patrons had significant economic and material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Part of a broad set of professional/social duties for intellectuals.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Durable professional identity throughout a philosopher's active career.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Occupation acquired through training and social recognition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Roman patronage norms for philosophers.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Quotes a character stating an established social 'duty'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Philosophy is not defined by attending dinner parties while ill.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical meals and gifts (portions) are tangible physical goods.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific practice within the patron-client system.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Part of a long-term professional relationship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Professional role/academic status is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes standard practice in the author's own society.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicit statement of a customary procedure ('would have been sent you').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Teaching philosophy does not inherently imply home delivery of food.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Physical space/seating at a banquet has social and material weight.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the arrangement of household dinning space.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Momentary arrangement for a single event.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth/class is a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Contemporary Lucianic satire of social norms.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Dialogue depicts a realistic exercise of paternal/master's authority.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealthy status doesn't tautologically require family displacement.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Restriction to specific physical quarters is a limitation on freedom of movement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Fundamental social structure (domestic segregation).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Usually a lifelong restriction for women in this cultural context.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is a biologically fixed/strictly immutable category.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the gynaikonitis, a standard feature of contemporary houses.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Casual reference to a factual architectural and social norm.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Women are not defined by the rooms they eat in.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Inheritance of gold, silver, and property are tangible material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis: the right to inherit.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Property ownership gained through inheritance is lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/legal personhood is a hard-to-change legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Roman/Greek testamentary law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reflects the factual legal mechanism of the period.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizen status does not define the receipt of a specific legacy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Mode of address and naming is a symbolic/status-based resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring across many social interactions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status and name-rights hold while one belongs to that wealth bracket.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Pauper status is durable but can change with wealth.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: A satire of contemporary social mobility and name-changing.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a socially typical (if dramatized) encounter.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A pauper's definition doesn't inherently mention short vs. long names.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Property and total estates are concrete material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational axis: property and inheritance rights.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inherited wealth is a lifelong structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Kinship/lineage is biologically and legally immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes factual contemporary intestate laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the law plainly ('Simon's by law').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a cousin doesn't define inheriting; it is the legal rule that links them.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Financial payment of taxes is a direct material burden/resource loss.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad category of civic financial liability (liturgies).
scores.persistence_reasoning: Durable tax class status while one remains wealthy.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the system of liturgies in force in the author's world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a factual civic obligation for the wealthy.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Richness is defined by having money, not by the legal duty to pay a 'special tax'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bodily safety from injury or death in battle is a concrete material resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to military service and command roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the period of active military duty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket determines the type of military service.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary military-social organization.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Contrasts known factual duties of different census classes.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth does not define 'risking one's life in the van'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to vote is a concrete legal right with structural effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis: political power.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Voting rights are a lifelong feature of citizenship.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Poverty status is durable; assembly attendance is a recurring role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the democratic process of the Classical/Imperial city.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a factual political right ('one of the voters').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being poor is not defined by voting in an assembly.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to speak determines legal and political outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: General right of legal personhood and political participation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Pertains to single sessions or instances of the assembly.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable social status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the dynamics of the assembly in the author's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Factual description of social dynamics, though stylistically heightened.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth is not defined by silence in an assembly.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Property ownership and its loss (confiscation) are material outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational axis: protection of wealth/property.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Confiscation results in a permanent loss of structural status.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Factual possibility of confiscation in contemporary law.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States a factual power of the assembly/public vote.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Wealth is the prerequisite, not the same as having it confiscated.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Baths and shows are tangible physical and social services.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific category of public amenities.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Recurring access to services throughout one's life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Wealth bracket is a durable social status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the liturgy-funded amenities of contemporary cities.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Accurately reflects the funding model of ancient public life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being rich does not define the funding of 'baths and shows'.
